**[1. Cover Letter]{.underline}**

December 9, 2021

Dr. Rachel Okonkwo Managing Member / CEO Arcadia Health Systems, LLC 4500 Medical Parkway, Suite 210 San Antonio, TX 78229

Re: Proposal for NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module --- MCS-PROP-2021-0487

Dear Dr. Okonkwo:

On behalf of Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc. (\"Meridian\"), I am pleased to present this comprehensive proposal for the deployment of our NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module to Arcadia Health Systems, LLC (\"Arcadia\"). This proposal reflects the discussions initiated during our October 12, 2021, introductory presentation and refined through the follow-up demonstration session on November 18, 2021, which included your senior leadership team and technical staff.

We have carefully considered Arcadia\'s stated objectives --- including the replacement of your current HealthBridge 4.0 analytics platform, the need for scalable reporting across your network of hospitals and clinics in Texas and Louisiana, and the desire for seamless interoperability with your existing electronic health record (\"EHR\") infrastructure. We believe NexusCore is uniquely positioned to address these requirements.

This proposal outlines Meridian\'s recommended solution, including a detailed technical description of the NexusCore platform, a phased implementation methodology, a comprehensive pricing structure, and the assumptions and dependencies upon which this proposal is based. The total proposed investment of \$14,700,000 reflects a three-year software license, implementation services, training, first-year maintenance and support, and data migration advisory consulting.

We look forward to the opportunity to discuss this proposal in detail and to advance toward a formal engagement. Please do not hesitate to contact me or our Account Executive, James Poletti, with any questions.

Respectfully submitted,

MERIDIAN CLOUD SOLUTIONS, INC.

By: **\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Name: James Poletti

Title: Account Executive, Healthcare Division

Email: j.poletti@meridiancloud.com

Phone: (512) 555-0193

**[2. Executive Summary]{.underline}**

Arcadia Health Systems, LLC is a growing regional healthcare IT services provider currently operating electronic health record integration and analytics platforms for 23 hospitals and over 140 clinics across Texas and Louisiana. With annual revenue of approximately \$62 million, Arcadia has established itself as a critical technology partner for mid-market healthcare organizations in the Gulf Coast region.

Arcadia has identified an urgent need to replace its legacy analytics platform, HealthBridge 4.0, which was originally deployed in 2016 and has reached the limits of its scalability, reporting flexibility, and ability to support modern data interoperability standards. Arcadia\'s leadership team has communicated the following strategic priorities:

> • **Modernization of Analytics Infrastructure.** Arcadia requires a cloud-native analytics platform capable of processing large-scale clinical and operational data sets in near real-time, replacing the batch-processing architecture of HealthBridge 4.0.
>
> • **Multi-Facility Scalability.** The platform must support Arcadia\'s current network of 23 hospitals and 140+ clinics and accommodate anticipated growth over the next five years.
>
> • **EHR Interoperability.** The solution must integrate with the diverse array of EHR systems deployed across Arcadia\'s client facilities, including platforms from multiple vendors, through standards-based API connectivity.
>
> • **Regulatory Compliance.** All data handling, storage, and reporting must comply with HIPAA, HITECH, and applicable state privacy regulations.
>
> • **Operational Efficiency.** Arcadia seeks measurable improvements in reporting cycle times, data accuracy, and staff productivity. Internal targets include a 30-40% improvement in reporting efficiency relative to HealthBridge 4.0.

**Meridian\'s Proposed Solution.** Meridian proposes the deployment of the NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module, a component of Meridian\'s flagship NexusCore cloud ERP platform. NexusCore is a modular, cloud-native platform purpose-built for enterprise-scale data analytics and operational intelligence. The Healthcare Analytics Module provides specialized functionality for clinical data aggregation, population health analytics, financial performance reporting, and regulatory compliance dashboarding tailored to the healthcare sector.

**Key Benefits.** NexusCore delivers industry-leading performance for healthcare analytics. Our clients typically see 30-40% improvement in reporting efficiency, reduced manual data reconciliation effort, and enhanced visibility into clinical and operational performance metrics. NexusCore\'s open API framework is designed to integrate with major EHR platforms through our standard API framework, subject to proper configuration and the availability of compliant data interfaces from the EHR vendors.

**Investment Overview.** The total proposed contract value is \$14,700,000, encompassing a three-year software license, Meridian-led implementation services, first-year maintenance and support, comprehensive training, and data migration advisory consulting. Additional detail on pricing is provided in Section 8 of this proposal.

**Implementation Timeline.** Meridian proposes a phased implementation commencing upon contract execution, with a target Go-Live date approximately six months following the project kickoff, subject to the assumptions and dependencies set forth in Section 9.

**[3. About Meridian Cloud Solutions]{.underline}**

Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc. was founded in 2011 and is headquartered in Austin, Texas. Meridian is a Delaware C-Corporation publicly traded on the NASDAQ under the ticker symbol \"MCSI,\" with annual revenue of approximately \$380 million. Meridian employs over 1,800 professionals across offices in Austin, Dallas, Atlanta, Boston, and San Francisco.

**Core Platform --- NexusCore.** NexusCore is Meridian\'s flagship modular cloud ERP and data analytics platform, serving mid-market and enterprise clients across healthcare, financial services, and logistics sectors. NexusCore is deployed across more than 300 enterprise clients, including regional health systems, multi-hospital networks, and specialty care organizations.

**Healthcare Vertical Expertise.** Meridian\'s Healthcare Division currently supports 74 healthcare clients across the United States, ranging from single-facility specialty practices to multi-state health systems managing over 50 hospital campuses. Our healthcare team includes over 200 implementation consultants, solution architects, and support engineers with deep domain expertise in clinical informatics, revenue cycle management, and healthcare regulatory compliance.

**Security and Compliance.** Meridian maintains SOC 2 Type II certification, HITRUST CSF certification, and has completed independent third-party assessments for HIPAA compliance. All client data hosted on the NexusCore platform is encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3), and is stored in geographically redundant data centers located within the continental United States.

**Financial Stability.** Meridian\'s financial statements are prepared in accordance with U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles and are reviewed by Pinnacle Audit Group LLP. Meridian\'s current-year financial data is publicly available through its SEC filings.

**[4. Understanding Arcadia\'s Requirements]{.underline}**

Based on Meridian\'s discovery sessions with Arcadia\'s leadership and technical teams on October 12, 2021, and November 18, 2021, Meridian understands Arcadia\'s requirements as follows:

**4.1 Current State Assessment**

Arcadia currently relies on HealthBridge 4.0, a proprietary analytics platform deployed in 2016. Key limitations of the current environment include:

> • **Scalability Constraints.** HealthBridge 4.0 was architected for a maximum of 15 concurrent hospital connections. Arcadia\'s current footprint of 23 hospitals exceeds this design threshold, resulting in degraded query performance and periodic system timeouts during peak reporting periods.
>
> • **Batch Processing Architecture.** HealthBridge 4.0 relies on overnight batch processing for data ingestion, resulting in a minimum 24-hour data latency for operational and clinical reports.
>
> • **Limited API Support.** HealthBridge 4.0 supports only proprietary data exchange formats and does not natively support HL7 FHIR, CDA, or modern REST API standards, requiring costly custom middleware for each EHR connection.
>
> • **Aging Codebase.** The HealthBridge 4.0 platform is built on a technology stack that is approaching end-of-life for several critical components, creating increasing maintenance burden and security risk.

**4.2 Desired Future State**

Arcadia has articulated the following requirements for a replacement analytics platform:

1\. Cloud-native architecture with horizontal scalability to support 50+ hospital connections and 500+ clinic endpoints.

2\. Near real-time data ingestion with latency of no more than 15 minutes for priority data feeds.

3\. Standards-based API connectivity (HL7 FHIR R4, REST, SOAP) for EHR integration.

4\. Pre-built healthcare analytics content including population health dashboards, quality measure reporting (HEDIS, CMS Star Ratings), and financial performance analysis.

5\. Role-based access control and comprehensive audit logging compliant with HIPAA and HITECH requirements.

6\. Self-service reporting capabilities allowing Arcadia\'s end users to build custom reports without developer intervention.

7\. Comprehensive training program for Arcadia\'s technical and business user communities.

8\. Data migration support --- advisory consulting and technical guidance for the transition from HealthBridge 4.0 to the new platform.

**4.3 Scope Boundaries**

Based on discussions during the November 18, 2021, demonstration, the parties have agreed on the following scope boundaries for this proposal:

> • **In Scope:** NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module; system configuration; user interface customization; report template development; API integration with Arcadia\'s existing EHR systems; training; data migration advisory consulting.
>
> • **Out of Scope:** Physical data migration execution (extraction, transformation, and loading of legacy data); Arcadia\'s internal network infrastructure upgrades; EHR vendor-side configuration changes; third-party systems integration services.

Arcadia has indicated its intent to retain a qualified third-party systems integrator for data migration and related activities. Meridian recommends that Arcadia engage such a partner early in the implementation process to ensure alignment with the overall project timeline.

**[5. Proposed Solution: NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module]{.underline}**

**5.1 Platform Overview**

NexusCore is a multi-tenant, cloud-native enterprise platform built on a microservices architecture. The Healthcare Analytics Module is one of twelve available NexusCore modules, purpose-built for healthcare data aggregation, transformation, and analytics. Key capabilities include:

> • **Data Ingestion Engine.** Supports concurrent ingestion of structured and semi-structured data from multiple sources, including EHR systems, claims data, pharmacy records, lab results, and third-party data feeds. The engine supports HL7 FHIR R4, HL7 v2.x, CDA, X12 (837/835), and custom flat-file formats.
>
> • **Analytics and Reporting.** Pre-built analytics content library with over 200 healthcare-specific report templates, dashboards, and scorecards. Content areas include clinical quality, population health, financial performance, utilization management, and regulatory compliance. Self-service report builder with drag-and-drop functionality for business users.
>
> • **Clinical Data Repository.** Centralized, normalized data repository optimized for healthcare data models (including OMOP CDM compatibility). Supports longitudinal patient records across multiple facilities and EHR systems.
>
> • **Population Health Management.** Risk stratification algorithms, care gap identification, chronic disease management analytics, and predictive modeling capabilities.
>
> • **Regulatory and Compliance Reporting.** Pre-configured templates for HEDIS, CMS Star Ratings, MIPS/APM quality reporting, and state-specific regulatory submissions.
>
> • **Security and Access Control.** Role-based and attribute-based access control, patient-level data segmentation, comprehensive audit logging, and break-the-glass emergency access protocols. Full HIPAA and HITECH compliance.

**5.2 Modular Architecture**

NexusCore\'s modular architecture allows Arcadia to deploy the Healthcare Analytics Module as a standalone solution while retaining the option to add additional NexusCore modules (e.g., Revenue Cycle Management, Supply Chain, HR/Workforce Management) in the future without re-platforming.

**5.3 Performance and Scalability**

The NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module is designed to support enterprise-scale deployments:

> • **Concurrent Users:** Up to 5,000 named users per instance.
>
> • **Data Volume:** Tested to 50 TB of active analytical data per tenant.
>
> • **Query Performance:** Sub-10-second response time for standard dashboard queries on datasets up to 10 TB (based on Meridian\'s standard benchmark environment; actual performance may vary depending on query complexity, data volume, and network conditions).
>
> • **Uptime SLA:** 99.9% platform availability, measured monthly, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.

**5.4 Integration Capabilities**

NexusCore provides a comprehensive API framework for integration with third-party systems:

> • **Standard Connectors.** Pre-built connectors for 18 major EHR platforms, including those most commonly deployed in the Texas and Louisiana healthcare markets. Meridian\'s standard API framework is designed to integrate with major EHR platforms, subject to proper configuration and the provision of compliant data interfaces from the EHR vendors.
>
> • **Custom Connector Toolkit.** For EHR systems not covered by pre-built connectors, Meridian provides a Custom Connector Toolkit enabling development of purpose-built interfaces. Custom connector development is included within the scope of Meridian\'s proposed implementation services (see Section 7).
>
> • **Data Specifications Requirement.** Successful API integration requires that the Licensee provide complete, accurate API specifications and access to test environments for each EHR system to be connected. Meridian\'s responsibility is limited to building and configuring API connectors in accordance with the specifications provided.

**[6. Technical Architecture and Integration Approach]{.underline}**

**6.1 Deployment Model**

NexusCore will be deployed as a dedicated tenant within Meridian\'s cloud infrastructure, hosted in Meridian\'s primary data center in Dallas, Texas, with failover capability to a secondary data center in Atlanta, Georgia. Arcadia\'s data will be logically isolated from other tenants through Meridian\'s multi-tenant isolation framework.

**6.2 Integration Architecture**

The proposed integration architecture for Arcadia consists of the following components:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Layer**               **Component**                     **Description**
  ----------------------- --------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------
  Ingestion               NexusCore Data Ingestion Engine   Receives data from EHR systems via API connectors

  Transformation          NexusCore ETL Pipeline            Normalizes, cleanses, and transforms ingested data

  Storage                 Clinical Data Repository          Centralized, OMOP-compatible data store

  Analytics               NexusCore Analytics Engine        Executes queries, aggregations, and predictive models

  Presentation            NexusCore Dashboard Framework     Delivers reports, dashboards, and scorecards to end users

  Security                NexusCore Security Layer          Manages authentication, authorization, encryption, and audit
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**6.3 Data Migration Approach**

As noted in Section 4.3, physical data migration --- the extraction, transformation, and loading of data from HealthBridge 4.0 into NexusCore --- is outside the scope of Meridian\'s proposed services. Meridian recommends that Arcadia retain a qualified third-party systems integrator experienced in healthcare data migration to perform these activities.

Meridian will provide data migration advisory consulting services, which include:

> • Detailed documentation of NexusCore data models, schemas, and import specifications
>
> • Technical guidance on data mapping from HealthBridge 4.0 data structures to NexusCore data models
>
> • Validation rules and quality checks for migrated data
>
> • Consultation sessions with Arcadia\'s selected systems integrator (up to 120 hours of advisory time)
>
> • Review and validation of sample data loads during the migration process

**Arcadia shall be solely responsible for all data migration activities, including the selection and management of any third-party systems integrator, the extraction of data from HealthBridge 4.0, the transformation of data to conform to NexusCore specifications, and the loading of data into the NexusCore platform. Meridian shall have no responsibility for the accuracy, completeness, or integrity of migrated data.**

**[7. Implementation Methodology and Timeline]{.underline}**

**7.1 Implementation Methodology**

Meridian employs a structured, phased implementation methodology for NexusCore deployments, consisting of five phases:

**Phase 1 --- Discovery and Planning (Weeks 1-4)**

> • Detailed requirements workshops with Arcadia\'s business and technical stakeholders
>
> • System architecture design and environment provisioning
>
> • Project plan development, including milestone schedule and resource allocation
>
> • Identification and documentation of all EHR integration requirements
>
> • Arcadia to designate a Project Manager with decision-making authority

**Phase 2 --- Configuration and Customization (Weeks 5-12)**

> • System configuration based on requirements gathered in Phase 1
>
> • User interface customization to align with Arcadia\'s branding and workflow requirements
>
> • Development of custom report templates and dashboards
>
> • API connector development and unit testing
>
> • Arcadia to provide complete API specifications and access to EHR test environments

**Phase 3 --- Integration and Data Validation (Weeks 13-18)**

> • End-to-end API integration testing with Arcadia\'s EHR systems
>
> • Data flow validation and reconciliation
>
> • Coordination with Arcadia\'s systems integrator on data migration activities (advisory capacity)
>
> • Security and compliance validation, including penetration testing and HIPAA compliance review

**Phase 4 --- User Acceptance Testing and Training (Weeks 19-22)**

> • Deployment of the platform to Arcadia\'s UAT environment
>
> • Structured User Acceptance Testing per mutually agreed test scripts
>
> • Comprehensive training program (see Section 7.3)
>
> • Remediation of defects identified during UAT

**Phase 5 --- Go-Live and Stabilization (Weeks 23-26)**

> • Production deployment
>
> • Go-Live support with dedicated Meridian on-site team
>
> • 30-day post-Go-Live hypercare period
>
> • Transition to standard maintenance and support

**7.2 Proposed Timeline**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Milestone**                                  **Target Date**                 **Notes**
  ---------------------------------------------- ------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------
  Contract Execution                             March 2022 (estimated)          Subject to negotiation

  Phase 1: Discovery & Planning Start            Contract Execution + 1 week     

  Phase 1 Complete                               Contract Execution + 4 weeks    

  Phase 2: Config & Customization Start          Week 5                          

  Arcadia Provides API Specifications            No later than Week 6            Critical dependency

  Phase 2 Complete                               Week 12                         

  Phase 3: Integration & Data Validation Start   Week 13                         

  Phase 3 Complete                               Week 18                         

  Phase 4: UAT & Training Start                  Week 19                         

  Phase 4 Complete                               Week 22                         

  Phase 5: Go-Live                               Week 23 (\~September 1, 2022)   Target date, subject to assumptions in Section 9

  Post-Go-Live Stabilization Complete            Week 26                         
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**7.3 Training Program**

Meridian\'s proposed training program includes:

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Training Track**                **Audience**                  **Format**                               **Duration**
  --------------------------------- ----------------------------- ---------------------------------------- -------------------
  Platform Administration           Arcadia IT staff              On-site instructor-led                   5 days

  Report Development                Arcadia analytics team        On-site instructor-led + virtual lab     4 days

  End User / Dashboard Navigation   Arcadia business users        Virtual instructor-led (cohorts of 25)   2 days per cohort

  API Management & Monitoring       Arcadia integration team      On-site instructor-led                   3 days

  Train-the-Trainer                 Arcadia designated trainers   On-site instructor-led                   3 days
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Total estimated training effort: 480 person-hours of instructor-led training, plus access to Meridian\'s online learning portal for 12 months following Go-Live.

**[8. Pricing Summary]{.underline}**

**8.1 Pricing Overview**

The total proposed contract value for the NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module deployment is **\$14,700,000**, broken down as follows:

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Line Item**                               **Description**                                                                                                                                                                       **Amount**
  ------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- -----------------------
  Software License Fees                       3-year subscription license for NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module, up to 2,500 named users                                                                                        \$6,200,000

  Implementation Services                     Meridian-scope implementation per SOW (Phases 1-5), including system configuration, customization, API connector development, integration testing, UAT support, and Go-Live support   \$3,800,000

  Annual Maintenance & Support (Year 1)       24/7 technical support, software updates, patches, and platform monitoring for Year 1                                                                                                 \$1,900,000

  Training Services                           Comprehensive training program per Section 7.3, including on-site and virtual instruction, materials, and 12-month online portal access                                               \$1,200,000

  Data Migration Consulting (Advisory Only)   Up to 120 hours of advisory consulting on data migration, including schema documentation, mapping guidance, validation rules, and SI coordination                                     \$1,600,000

  **Total Proposed Contract Value**                                                                                                                                                                                                 **\$14,700,000**
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**8.2 Payment Schedule**

Meridian proposes the following payment schedule:

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Payment Milestone**   **Amount**              **Timing**
  ----------------------- ----------------------- --------------------------------------------------
  Contract Execution      \$3,675,000 (25%)       Upon execution of MSLSA

  Phase 1 Completion      \$2,205,000 (15%)       Upon completion of Discovery & Planning

  Phase 2 Completion      \$2,940,000 (20%)       Upon completion of Configuration & Customization

  Go-Live                 \$2,940,000 (20%)       Upon Go-Live deployment

  Final Payment           \$2,940,000 (20%)       30 days post-Go-Live

  **Total**               **\$14,700,000**        
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**8.3 Annual Recurring Costs (Years 2 and 3)**

Following Year 1, annual maintenance and support fees are subject to adjustment as follows:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Item**                       **Year 2**              **Year 3**
  ------------------------------ ----------------------- -----------------------
  Annual Maintenance & Support   \$1,995,000             \$2,094,750

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Annual maintenance and support fees increase by 5% per year. Renewal of maintenance and support beyond Year 3 will be subject to mutual agreement.

**8.4 Optional Add-On Services**

The following services are not included in the base contract value but are available as optional add-ons:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Service**                                         **Estimated Cost**                   **Notes**
  --------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------ -------------------------------------------------
  Additional API Connectors (per connector)           \$150,000 - \$200,000                For EHR systems not in original scope

  Custom Dashboard Development                        \$75,000 - \$150,000 per dashboard   Beyond the templates included in implementation

  Extended Training (per additional cohort)           \$35,000 per cohort                  Virtual instructor-led, 2 days

  On-Site Resident Engineer (per month)               \$28,000                             Post-Go-Live embedded support

  Additional Named User Licenses (per block of 500)   \$310,000 per year                   Incremental to base 2,500 users
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**8.5 Pricing Assumptions**

The pricing set forth in this proposal is based on the following assumptions:

1\. The scope of services is limited to those described in this proposal. Any changes, additions, or expansions of scope will require a mutually agreed Change Order with associated pricing adjustments.

2\. Arcadia will provide timely access to personnel, systems, data, and facilities as required for implementation.

3\. The number of EHR systems requiring API integration does not exceed ten (10) at the time of initial deployment. Additional connectors may be added via Change Order.

4\. Data migration activities (extraction, transformation, loading) are performed by Arcadia or its designated third-party systems integrator, not by Meridian.

5\. Arcadia\'s network infrastructure meets the minimum technical requirements specified in Appendix A.

6\. Training is delivered in English.

**[9. Assumptions and Dependencies]{.underline}**

The proposed implementation timeline, pricing, and scope are contingent upon the following assumptions and dependencies. Deviation from these assumptions may result in schedule adjustments, scope changes, and/or pricing modifications, to be addressed through the Change Order process.

**9.1 Arcadia Obligations and Dependencies**

1\. **Project Manager.** Arcadia shall designate a Project Manager with appropriate decision-making authority no later than five (5) business days following contract execution. The Project Manager shall serve as the primary point of contact for all implementation-related matters and shall be authorized to approve deliverables, milestone completions, and day-to-day project decisions on Arcadia\'s behalf. Failure to designate or maintain a Project Manager may result in project delays attributable to Arcadia.

2\. **Subject Matter Experts.** Arcadia shall make subject matter experts available for requirements gathering workshops, data validation sessions, and UAT activities as reasonably requested by Meridian\'s implementation team. Meridian estimates a requirement of 4-6 Arcadia SMEs during Phase 1 and 2-3 SMEs during Phases 2 through 5.

3\. **API Specifications.** Arcadia shall provide complete, accurate API specifications and technical documentation for each EHR system to be integrated, no later than Week 6 of the implementation timeline (see Section 7.2). Late delivery of API specifications is the single most common cause of implementation delay in healthcare platform deployments and may result in corresponding delays to the Go-Live date.

4\. **Test Environments.** Arcadia shall provide access to EHR test environments, with representative data sets, for integration testing purposes. Test environments must be available no later than Week 10 of the implementation timeline.

5\. **Data Migration.** Arcadia shall be solely responsible for all data migration activities, including the retention and management of a qualified third-party systems integrator. Meridian strongly recommends that Arcadia engage its systems integrator no later than the commencement of Phase 2 (Week 5) to allow adequate time for coordination. Delays in data migration activities caused by Arcadia or its systems integrator shall not be attributable to Meridian.

6\. **Infrastructure Readiness.** Arcadia shall ensure that its network infrastructure, including bandwidth, firewall configurations, and VPN connectivity, meets the minimum technical requirements set forth in Appendix A prior to the commencement of Phase 3 (Week 13).

7\. **User Acceptance Testing.** Arcadia shall complete User Acceptance Testing within the timeframe specified in the final contract. Failure to complete UAT or to provide timely written notice of material nonconformity within the contractually specified period may result in deemed acceptance of the delivered system.

**9.2 Meridian Obligations**

1\. Meridian shall assign a dedicated Implementation Lead and project team with appropriate expertise in healthcare analytics deployments.

2\. Meridian shall deliver all in-scope implementation services in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with industry standards.

3\. Meridian shall provide timely responses to Arcadia\'s technical inquiries and change requests.

4\. Meridian shall deliver software that performs substantially in accordance with the applicable Documentation for the warranty period specified in the final contract.

**9.3 Shared Assumptions**

1\. Both parties shall participate in weekly project status meetings throughout the implementation.

2\. The implementation timeline assumes no material changes to Arcadia\'s EHR environment or business requirements during the implementation period.

3\. All scope changes shall be documented through mutually executed Change Orders in accordance with the final contract\'s change management procedures.

4\. Force majeure events, including but not limited to natural disasters, pandemics, governmental actions, and telecommunications failures, may extend the implementation timeline without fault to either party.

**9.4 Risk Factors**

Meridian has identified the following risk factors that may affect the implementation timeline or project outcomes:

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Risk**                                              **Likelihood**    **Impact**                      **Mitigation**
  ----------------------------------------------------- ----------------- ------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------
  Late delivery of API specifications by Arcadia        Medium-High       High (direct schedule impact)   Early engagement; specification templates provided in Phase 1

  Data quality issues in legacy HealthBridge 4.0 data   Medium            Medium-High                     Data profiling in Phase 1; validation rules in advisory consulting

  Arcadia Project Manager turnover                      Low-Medium        High (decision-making gaps)     Backup PM designation recommended

  Third-party SI (data migration) coordination delays   Medium            Medium                          Early SI engagement; regular coordination meetings

  Scope expansion during implementation                 Medium            Medium                          Formal Change Order process

  EHR vendor non-cooperation on test environments       Low               Medium                          Early vendor outreach recommended
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[10. Proposed Contractual Framework]{.underline}**

Meridian proposes that the parties enter into the following agreements to govern this engagement:

**10.1 Master Software License and Services Agreement (\"MSLSA\")**

The MSLSA will serve as the overarching agreement governing the software license grant, service delivery, intellectual property rights, confidentiality, warranties, limitations of liability, indemnification, and general terms and conditions.

Key commercial terms proposed for the MSLSA include:

> • **License Grant.** Non-exclusive, non-transferable subscription license for the NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module for the term specified.
>
> • **License Term.** Three (3) years from the Go-Live Date, with automatic renewal for successive one (1) year periods unless terminated with 180 days\' prior written notice.
>
> • **Limited Warranty.** Meridian warrants that the software will perform substantially in accordance with the Documentation for a period of ninety (90) days following Go-Live. The sole remedy for breach of this warranty will be repair, replacement, or, if Meridian is unable to cure, a pro-rata refund of prepaid license fees.
>
> • **Warranty Disclaimer.** Except for the limited warranty, Meridian disclaims all other warranties, express or implied, including implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement.
>
> • **Limitation of Liability.** Neither party\'s aggregate liability under the MSLSA shall exceed the total fees paid or payable during the twelve (12) month period immediately preceding the event giving rise to such liability.
>
> • **Consequential Damages Waiver.** Neither party shall be liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, punitive, or exemplary damages, including loss of profits, goodwill, use, data, or other intangible losses.
>
> • **Exclusive Remedies.** The remedies set forth in the MSLSA shall be the Licensee\'s sole and exclusive remedies for any breach.
>
> • **Integration Clause.** The MSLSA, together with all Exhibits, Schedules, and Statements of Work, will constitute the entire agreement between the parties, superseding all prior agreements, proposals, representations, and understandings, whether oral or written.
>
> • **Governing Law.** Delaware law, without regard to conflict of law provisions.
>
> • **Forum Selection.** Exclusive jurisdiction in federal or state courts located in Austin, Travis County, Texas.

**10.2 Statement of Work #1 (\"SOW-1\")**

SOW-1 will describe the detailed scope of Meridian\'s implementation services, milestones, deliverables, acceptance criteria, and the respective obligations of Meridian and Arcadia. SOW-1 will be executed concurrently with the MSLSA and attached as an exhibit.

Key provisions proposed for SOW-1 include:

> • Detailed description of Meridian\'s implementation scope (Phases 1-5)
>
> • Milestone schedule with target dates
>
> • Acceptance testing procedures and timelines
>
> • Arcadia\'s obligation to designate a Project Manager, provide SMEs, deliver API specifications, and make test environments available
>
> • Clear delineation that **data migration is Arcadia\'s sole responsibility**, with Meridian providing advisory consulting only
>
> • Change Order procedures for scope modifications

**10.3 Change Order Process**

Any changes to the scope, timeline, or pricing of services described in SOW-1 or subsequent Statements of Work shall require a mutually executed Change Order signed by authorized representatives of both parties. Change Orders may affect the implementation timeline, Go-Live date, and/or contract value.

**[11. Terms, Conditions, and Disclaimers]{.underline}**

**11.1 Proposal Validity**

This proposal is valid through March 31, 2022. If a definitive agreement is not executed by that date, Meridian reserves the right to modify or withdraw this proposal.

**11.2 Disclaimers. IMPORTANT --- PLEASE READ CAREFULLY.**

**Estimated timelines and performance metrics are provided for planning purposes only and do not constitute guarantees. Actual results may vary based on client environment, data quality, and implementation decisions.**

The statements of capability, performance metrics, timeline estimates, and projected outcomes contained in this proposal are based on Meridian\'s experience with comparable deployments and are intended to provide Arcadia with a reasonable basis for evaluating the proposed solution. They are not, and should not be construed as, warranties, guarantees, or binding commitments regarding the specific results that Arcadia will achieve.

Without limiting the generality of the foregoing:

1\. **Performance Metrics.** References to reporting efficiency improvements (e.g., \"30-40% improvement\") are based on aggregated data from Meridian\'s existing client base and reflect typical results observed in comparable healthcare analytics deployments. Individual client results depend on numerous factors outside Meridian\'s control, including but not limited to data quality, system configuration, user adoption, infrastructure capacity, and organizational readiness. Meridian does not warrant or guarantee that Arcadia will achieve any specific level of performance improvement.

2\. **Timeline Estimates.** The implementation timeline set forth in Section 7.2 is a good-faith estimate based on the assumptions and dependencies described in Section 9. Actual timelines may differ materially from estimates depending on factors including, without limitation, the timely performance of Arcadia\'s obligations, the performance of third-party systems integrators, the availability and quality of legacy data, and the scope of customization and integration required.

3\. **Integration Statements.** Statements regarding NexusCore\'s ability to integrate with EHR platforms refer to the platform\'s architectural capability to support such integrations through its standard API framework and Custom Connector Toolkit. Successful integration with any specific EHR system depends on multiple factors, including the availability of compliant data interfaces from the EHR vendor, the provision of accurate API specifications by Arcadia, and the proper configuration and testing of connectors during implementation. Meridian does not warrant that integration with any particular EHR system will be seamless, error-free, or achievable within any specific timeframe.

4\. **Proposal vs. Contract.** This proposal is a preliminary document intended to facilitate discussions between the parties. It does not constitute a binding agreement, and the terms and conditions of the engagement will be governed exclusively by the definitive MSLSA, SOW-1, and any associated exhibits and schedules as executed by the parties. In the event of any conflict between this proposal and the executed MSLSA, the MSLSA shall control.

5\. **No Reliance.** Arcadia should not rely solely on the information in this proposal in making its procurement decision. Meridian encourages Arcadia to conduct independent due diligence, including reference calls with existing Meridian clients, proof-of-concept evaluations, and technical assessments, to validate the suitability of NexusCore for Arcadia\'s specific requirements and environment.

**11.3 Confidentiality.** This proposal contains proprietary and confidential information of Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc. Arcadia agrees to treat this proposal as confidential and not to disclose its contents to any third party without Meridian\'s prior written consent, except to Arcadia\'s employees, officers, directors, advisors, and counsel who have a need to know for purposes of evaluating this proposal.

**11.4 Intellectual Property**

All intellectual property rights in the NexusCore platform, including all software, documentation, tools, methodologies, and pre-existing materials, are and shall remain the exclusive property of Meridian. Nothing in this proposal grants Arcadia any rights in Meridian\'s intellectual property except as may be expressly provided in the definitive MSLSA.

**11.5 Non-Solicitation.** During the term of any engagement arising from this proposal and for twelve (12) months thereafter, neither party shall directly solicit for employment any employee of the other party who was involved in the performance of the engagement, without the prior written consent of the employing party.

**[Appendix A --- NexusCore Technical Specifications Summary]{.underline}**

**Minimum Client Infrastructure Requirements**

For optimal performance of the NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module, Arcadia\'s network infrastructure should meet or exceed the following minimum specifications:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  Authentication                      SAML 2.0 compatible identity provider for single sign-on integration

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**NexusCore Platform Specifications**

  ----------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  Hosting                             Multi-tenant SaaS; primary DC in Dallas, TX; failover DC in Atlanta, GA

  Data Encryption                     AES-256 at rest; TLS 1.3 in transit

  Compliance Certifications           SOC 2 Type II, HITRUST CSF, HIPAA BAA available

  Availability SLA                    99.9% monthly uptime (excluding scheduled maintenance)

  Backup                              Daily incremental, weekly full; 90-day retention; geo-redundant storage

  Disaster Recovery                   RPO: 1 hour; RTO: 4 hours

  Maximum Named Users                 5,000 per instance (expandable)

  Maximum Active Data Volume          50 TB per tenant

  Standard Query Response             Sub-10-second for standard dashboards on datasets up to 10 TB

  Supported Browsers                  Chrome, Edge, Firefox (current and prior major version)

  Mobile Access                       Responsive web interface; native iOS and Android apps (read-only dashboards)
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[Appendix B --- Representative Client References]{.underline}**

Meridian is pleased to provide the following representative client references for Arcadia\'s due diligence. Contact information will be provided upon request, subject to client consent.

**Reference 1: San Marcos Regional Health**

>

>

**Reference 2: Coastal Bend Medical Group**

> • **Organization:** Coastal Bend Medical Group (fictional)

>
> • **NexusCore Module(s):** Healthcare Analytics

> • **Deployment Year:** 2020
>
> • **Scope:** NexusCore Healthcare Analytics deployment for clinical quality reporting and financial performance dashboards; integration with 2 EHR platforms
>
> • **Results:** Reported near real-time data availability (reduced latency from 24 hours to under 15 minutes); self-service reporting capability adopted by 80% of target user base within 6 months of Go-Live; identified \$1.2 million in revenue leakage through enhanced financial analytics

result:
**MERIDIAN CLOUD SOLUTIONS, INC.**

**PROJECT STATUS REPORT**

**Project NexusCore --- Arcadia Health Systems Implementation** **Report Reference: PSR-2022-0825**

**Report Date:** August 25, 2022 **Prepared By:** Implementation Services Division **Distribution:** Internal --- Restricted

**[1. REPORT SUMMARY]{.underline}**

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Field**                           **Detail**
  ----------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Project Name**                    NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module --- Arcadia Health Systems

  **Contract Reference**              Master Software License and Services Agreement dated March 15, 2022; Statement of Work #1 (\"SOW-1\")

  **Client**                          Arcadia Health Systems, LLC

  **Meridian Project Lead**           Ryan Tanaka, Senior Implementation Manager

  **Meridian Executive Sponsor**      Sandra Bledsoe, VP of Customer Success

  **Client Project Manager**          Kevin Liu (departed April 28, 2022) / *Vacant April 28 -- June 12, 2022* / Priya Nair (designated June 12, 2022)

  **Third-Party Integrator**          Linden Park Consulting, LLC (retained by Arcadia, effective July 1, 2022)

  **Original Go-Live Target**         September 1, 2022

  **Revised Go-Live Estimate**        January 15, 2023 (proposed; subject to mitigation plan execution)

  **Overall Project Status**          **RED --- Critical Path Impacted**
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[2. EXECUTIVE OVERVIEW]{.underline}**

This report provides a comprehensive assessment of the NexusCore implementation for Arcadia Health Systems, LLC (\"Arcadia\") as of August 25, 2022. The project is currently tracking **137 calendar days behind the original Go-Live target of September 1, 2022**, and the Implementation Services team has concluded that the original Go-Live date is no longer achievable. This report identifies three primary root causes for the delay, each of which is attributable to client-side or third-party dependencies outside Meridian\'s scope of control. A revised timeline with a proposed Go-Live date of January 15, 2023, is presented below, together with risk mitigation actions and escalation recommendations.

All three root causes were previously escalated to the client through weekly status meeting minutes (references: WSM-012 dated May 5, 2022; WSM-016 dated June 2, 2022; WSM-022 dated July 14, 2022; WSM-026 dated August 11, 2022). This report consolidates those escalations into a single formal document for internal executive review and project governance purposes.

**[3. OVERALL STATUS DASHBOARD]{.underline}**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Workstream**                     **Status**                  **Owner**                            **Notes**
  ---------------------------------- --------------------------- ------------------------------------ -----------------------------------------------
  Requirements Gathering             **GREEN --- Complete**      Meridian / Arcadia                   Completed June 30, 2022 (delayed from May 15)

  System Configuration               **AMBER --- In Progress**   Meridian                             70% complete; blocked on API specs

  UI Customization                   **GREEN --- On Track**      Meridian                             85% complete

  Report Template Development        **AMBER --- Delayed**       Meridian                             Dependent on configuration completion

  API Integration (EHR Connectors)   **RED --- Critical**        Meridian (build) / Arcadia (specs)   Blocked; see Root Cause #2

  Data Migration                     **RED --- Critical**        Arcadia / Linden Park Consulting     Script failures; see Root Cause #3

  Training                           **AMBER --- Not Started**   Meridian                             Cannot commence until configuration stable

  User Acceptance Testing            **RED --- Deferred**        Arcadia                              Dependent on all upstream workstreams
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Legend:**

> • **GREEN** --- On track or complete; no action required.
>
> • **AMBER** --- At risk; mitigation in progress; management attention needed.
>
> • **RED** --- Critical; off-track; executive escalation required.

**[4. ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS]{.underline}**

**4.1 Root Cause #1: Client Project Manager Designation Gap (45 Days)**

**Impact Rating:** HIGH

**Description:** Per Section 4(a) of SOW-1, Arcadia was required to \"designate a Project Manager with decision-making authority\" to serve as the primary point of coordination for the implementation. Arcadia\'s original Project Manager, Kevin Liu, departed Arcadia\'s employment on **April 28, 2022**, during the critical requirements-gathering phase. Arcadia did not designate a replacement Project Manager until **June 12, 2022**, when Priya Nair was assigned to the role --- a gap of **45 calendar days**.

**Impact to Schedule:** During the 45-day vacancy, Meridian\'s implementation team was unable to obtain timely decisions on requirements prioritization, configuration choices, or access authorizations. Specifically:

> • Fourteen (14) requirements decision requests submitted by Meridian between April 29 and June 11 went unanswered or were routed to personnel without decision-making authority.
>
> • Access to Arcadia\'s staging EHR environment was delayed by 22 days because no authorized client representative was available to approve Meridian\'s security credential request.
>
> • Two scheduled requirements workshops (May 4 and May 18) were cancelled by Arcadia due to the PM vacancy.

**Schedule Impact:** The 45-day PM gap directly caused a **38-day delay** to the requirements-gathering phase (accounting for partial overlap with other activities). This delay cascaded into the configuration and integration workstreams, as configuration cannot commence on modules where requirements remain unvalidated.

**Meridian\'s Mitigation Efforts:** Meridian escalated the PM vacancy issue in writing on May 5, 2022 (WSM-012), May 19, 2022 (email from Ryan Tanaka to Kevin Liu\'s former supervisor), and June 2, 2022 (WSM-016). Upon Ms. Nair\'s designation on June 12, Meridian conducted an accelerated requirements validation sprint over a two-week period to minimize further delay.

**4.2 Root Cause #2: Late Delivery of API Specifications by Arcadia (75 Days)**

**Impact Rating:** CRITICAL

**Description:** Section 3.1(d) of SOW-1 establishes a shared responsibility for API integration between NexusCore and Arcadia\'s existing EHR systems: Meridian is responsible for building the API connectors, and Arcadia is responsible for \"providing complete API specifications and access to test environments\" per the milestone schedule. The SOW-1 milestone schedule (Exhibit B) required Arcadia to deliver complete API specifications for all in-scope EHR systems by **May 6, 2022**.

Arcadia did not deliver the API specifications until **July 20, 2022** --- a delay of **75 calendar days** from the contractual milestone date.

**Impact to Schedule:** The API integration workstream is on the critical path. Without API specifications, Meridian\'s engineering team cannot design, build, or test the EHR connectors. The 75-day delay to this dependency has the following downstream effects:

> • API connector development, originally scheduled for May 7 -- July 15, 2022, cannot commence until specifications are reviewed and validated (estimated 2-week review period following July 20 delivery).
>
> • Integration testing, originally scheduled for July 16 -- August 15, 2022, is now estimated for November 2022 at the earliest.
>
> • User Acceptance Testing, which requires functional API integration, is deferred from August 2022 to December 2022 / January 2023.

**Contributing Factors:** The late API specification delivery appears to be connected to Root Cause #1. During the 45-day PM gap, Arcadia\'s technical staff indicated they lacked authorization to share certain system architecture documentation with Meridian. Additionally, Arcadia\'s CTO, Martin Schreiber, informed Meridian during the July 14 status meeting (WSM-022) that Arcadia\'s legacy HealthBridge 4.0 platform had undocumented API endpoints that required reverse-engineering before specifications could be finalized.

**Meridian\'s Mitigation Efforts:** Meridian submitted formal milestone reminder notices on May 6, May 20, June 6, and July 1, 2022. Meridian offered to provide supplemental API discovery consulting (outside the SOW-1 scope) at additional cost, but Arcadia declined. Following receipt of specifications on July 20, Meridian\'s API engineering team has been assigned to an accelerated review track.

**4.3 Root Cause #3: Linden Park Consulting Data Migration Script Errors**

**Impact Rating:** CRITICAL

**Description:** Per Section 3.2 of SOW-1, Arcadia is \"solely responsible for all data migration activities, including extraction, transformation, and loading of legacy data into NexusCore.\" Meridian\'s role is limited to providing \"advisory consulting services as described in Exhibit C\" and has \"no responsibility for the accuracy, completeness, or integrity of migrated data.\" Arcadia retained Linden Park Consulting, LLC (\"Linden Park\"), a third-party systems integrator based in Houston, Texas, on **July 1, 2022**, to perform data migration from the HealthBridge 4.0 platform to NexusCore.

On **August 12, 2022**, Linden Park executed its first full data migration test run into Meridian\'s NexusCore staging environment. The test revealed **critical errors** in Linden Park\'s extraction-transformation-load (\"ETL\") scripts:

> • **Data Type Mismatches:** Approximately 34% of migrated clinical encounter records contained incorrectly mapped data types, causing field-level validation failures in NexusCore\'s data ingestion layer.
>
> • **Referential Integrity Violations:** Patient-provider relationship tables lost foreign key associations during transformation, resulting in orphaned records.
>
> • **Character Encoding Errors:** Legacy HealthBridge 4.0 data stored in Windows-1252 encoding was not properly converted to UTF-8, causing corruption of special characters in approximately 12,000 patient records.
>
> • **Duplicate Record Creation:** The ETL script lacked deduplication logic, resulting in the creation of approximately 8,500 duplicate patient demographic records.

**Impact to Schedule:** Data migration is a prerequisite for meaningful User Acceptance Testing and Go-Live. The August 12 test run failure requires Linden Park to remediate its ETL scripts and re-execute the migration. Based on Linden Park\'s preliminary remediation timeline communicated to Arcadia on August 19, 2022, a corrected migration test run is not expected before **late October 2022**, with a production migration targeted for **December 2022**.

**Meridian\'s Advisory Role:** Meridian\'s data migration advisory consultant, Elena Vasquez, provided Linden Park with NexusCore\'s data import specifications, schema documentation, and field-mapping templates on July 15, 2022 --- within 14 days of Linden Park\'s engagement, as required by Exhibit C. Meridian\'s advisory scope does not include writing, reviewing, or validating Linden Park\'s ETL scripts.

**[5. ORIGINAL VS. REVISED PROJECT TIMELINE]{.underline}**

The following table presents the original milestone schedule (per SOW-1, Exhibit B) alongside the revised projected timeline based on the root cause analysis above.

**Gantt Summary --- Original vs. Revised Schedule:**

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **\#**   **Milestone**                            **Original Start**   **Original End**   **Revised Start**   **Revised End**   **Delta (Days)**   **Status**
  -------- ---------------------------------------- -------------------- ------------------ ------------------- ----------------- ------------------ -----------------
  M-01     Requirements Gathering                   Mar 16, 2022         May 15, 2022       Mar 16, 2022        Jun 30, 2022      +46                Complete

  M-02     System Configuration                     May 16, 2022         Jul 15, 2022       Jul 1, 2022         Oct 15, 2022      +92                In Progress

  M-03     UI Customization                         May 16, 2022         Jun 30, 2022       May 16, 2022        Aug 31, 2022      +62                In Progress

  M-04     Report Template Dev.                     Jun 1, 2022          Jul 31, 2022       Sep 1, 2022         Nov 15, 2022      +107               Not Started

  M-05     API Spec Delivery (Arcadia)              Mar 16, 2022         May 6, 2022        Mar 16, 2022        Jul 20, 2022      +75                Complete (Late)

  M-06     API Connector Build                      May 7, 2022          Jul 15, 2022       Aug 3, 2022         Nov 1, 2022       +109               Not Started

  M-07     Data Migration (Linden Park / Arcadia)   Jun 1, 2022          Aug 1, 2022        Jul 1, 2022         Dec 15, 2022      +136               Blocked

  M-08     Integration Testing                      Jul 16, 2022         Aug 15, 2022       Nov 2, 2022         Dec 20, 2022      +127               Deferred

  M-09     Training                                 Jul 1, 2022          Aug 15, 2022       Dec 1, 2022         Jan 10, 2023      +148               Deferred

  M-10     User Acceptance Testing                  Aug 1, 2022          Aug 31, 2022       Jan 1, 2023         Jan 14, 2023      +136               Deferred

  M-11     **Go-Live**                              **Sep 1, 2022**      ---                **Jan 15, 2023**    ---               **+137**           **Projected**
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Critical Path:** M-05 (API Specs) → M-06 (API Build) → M-08 (Integration Testing) → M-10 (UAT) → M-11 (Go-Live). The data migration workstream (M-07) runs in parallel but must converge with M-08 before integration testing can commence.

**[6. RISK REGISTER]{.underline}**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Risk ID**   **Description**                                                                                                                **Likelihood**   **Impact**   **Owner**               **Mitigation**
  ------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ ---------------- ------------ ----------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  R-001         Linden Park unable to remediate ETL scripts by October target                                                                  Medium           Critical     Arcadia / Linden Park   Meridian to offer optional data remediation services under Change Order (est. \$900,000)

  R-002         Additional undocumented API endpoints discovered in HealthBridge 4.0                                                           Medium           High         Arcadia                 Arcadia CTO to complete full legacy system audit by Sept 15, 2022

  R-003         Arcadia requests additional EHR system integrations not in original SOW-1 scope                                                High             Medium       Arcadia / Meridian      Any scope expansion requires formal Change Order per Section 5.4 of MSLSA

  R-004         Arcadia PM (Nair) lacks institutional knowledge of legacy systems                                                              Low              Medium       Arcadia                 Meridian to provide supplemental knowledge transfer sessions

  R-005         Revised Go-Live date of Jan 15, 2023 may slip further if Linden Park remediation is delayed                                    Medium           Critical     Arcadia / Linden Park   Weekly migration progress reviews; escalation to Arcadia CEO if milestones missed

  R-006         Contractual 30-day acceptance testing window (Section 5.3) may be insufficient if data quality issues persist post-migration   Low              High         Arcadia                 Arcadia should plan for extended parallel-run period outside of contractual UAT
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[7. ACTION ITEMS]{.underline}**

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **\#**         **Action**                                                                                                                                **Owner**                                **Due Date**         **Status**
  -------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------------------- -------------------- --------------
  AI-001         Escalate project status to Arcadia executive leadership (Dr. Okonkwo) with formal notice of Go-Live delay and root cause attribution      Sandra Bledsoe (Meridian)                August 30, 2022      Pending

  AI-002         Complete review and validation of API specifications received July 20                                                                     Meridian API Engineering                 September 9, 2022    In Progress

  AI-003         Request written confirmation from Arcadia regarding Linden Park\'s remediation timeline                                                   Ryan Tanaka (Meridian)                   September 1, 2022    Pending

  AI-004         Prepare draft Change Order for additional API connectors if Arcadia confirms additional legacy systems in scope                           Meridian Commercial Team                 September 15, 2022   Pending

  AI-005         Prepare draft Change Order for optional data remediation services (to address Linden Park ETL failures if Linden Park cannot remediate)   Meridian Commercial Team                 September 15, 2022   Pending

  AI-006         Conduct internal legal review of SOW-1 obligation allocation to confirm Meridian\'s position on data migration responsibility             Patricia M. Espinoza (General Counsel)   September 8, 2022    Pending

  AI-007         Archive all weekly status meeting minutes, milestone reminder notices, and client correspondence for project governance record            Ryan Tanaka (Meridian)                   Ongoing              In Progress
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[8. FINANCIAL SUMMARY]{.underline}**

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Category**                   **Original Contract Value**   **Spent to Date**   **Remaining**     **Notes**
  ------------------------------ ----------------------------- ------------------- ----------------- -----------------------------------------
  Software License Fees          \$6,200,000                   \$3,100,000         \$3,100,000       50% invoiced at signing; 50% at Go-Live

  Implementation Services        \$3,800,000                   \$2,470,000         \$1,330,000       Billed on milestone basis

  Maintenance & Support (Yr 1)   \$1,900,000                   \$0                 \$1,900,000       Commences at Go-Live

  Training                       \$1,200,000                   \$0                 \$1,200,000       Not yet commenced

  Data Migration Advisory        \$1,600,000                   \$960,000           \$640,000         60% delivered; advisory only

  **MSLSA Total**                **\$14,700,000**              **\$6,530,000**     **\$8,170,000**   

  Change Orders (Projected)      TBD                           \$0                 TBD               See Risk Register R-001, R-003
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[9. RECOMMENDATIONS]{.underline}**

Based on the analysis presented in this report, the Implementation Services team makes the following recommendations:

1\. **Formal Escalation to Arcadia Leadership.** Sandra Bledsoe should deliver a formal written notice to Dr. Rachel Okonkwo documenting the revised Go-Live date and the three root causes, with specific reference to Arcadia\'s contractual obligations under SOW-1, Sections 3.2 and 4. This communication should be reviewed by General Counsel prior to delivery.

2\. **Change Order Preparation.** The Commercial Team should prepare Change Orders in advance for (a) any additional API connectors required for legacy systems not identified in the original scope, and (b) optional data remediation services to address Linden Park\'s ETL script failures, estimated at \$900,000. These Change Orders should be executed per Section 5.4 of the MSLSA.

3\. **Documentation Preservation.** All project correspondence, status meeting minutes, milestone reminder notices, and Linden Park-related communications should be archived in Meridian\'s project governance repository. Given the magnitude of the schedule deviation and the potential for client dissatisfaction, a comprehensive documentation trail is essential.

4\. **Weekly Linden Park Migration Reviews.** Meridian should request that Arcadia provide weekly written updates on Linden Park\'s ETL remediation progress. If Linden Park cannot deliver a successful test migration by late October 2022, the revised Go-Live date of January 15, 2023, will be at risk.

5\. **Internal Legal Review.** General Counsel should review this report and the relevant contractual provisions to confirm Meridian\'s position regarding the allocation of responsibility for the Go-Live delay, particularly with respect to data migration obligations under SOW-1, Section 3.2.

**[10. APPROVAL]{.underline}**

This report has been reviewed and approved for internal distribution by the undersigned.

**Prepared By:**
