result:
**[MERIDIAN CLOUD SOLUTIONS, INC.]{.underline}**

**[NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module]{.underline}**

**[Post-Go-Live Support Ticket Summary Report]{.underline}**

**[March 1, 2023 --- July 31, 2023]{.underline}**

**Prepared for Stonebridge & Calloway LLP** **In Connection with Arcadia Health Systems, LLC v. Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.** **Case No. 1:23-cv-00847-CMA (W.D. Tex.)**

**Report Date:** November 22, 2023 **Prepared By:** Meridian Customer Support Engineering **Reviewed By:** Sandra Bledsoe, VP of Customer Success **Classification:** Confidential --- Litigation Hold / Attorney Work Product

**[1. Executive Summary]{.underline}**

This report provides a comprehensive summary of all forty-seven (47) support tickets logged by Arcadia Health Systems, LLC (\"Arcadia\" or \"Client\") between March 1, 2023, and July 31, 2023, following the Go-Live of Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.\'s (\"Meridian\") NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module on January 15, 2023. This report has been prepared at the request of outside litigation counsel, Stonebridge & Calloway LLP, in connection with the above-captioned matter.

**Key Findings:**

> • **Total Tickets Logged:** 47
>
> • **Tickets Resolved Within SLA:** 31 (65.96%)
>
> • **Tickets Attributable to Data Quality / Legacy Migration Issues:** 12 (25.53%)
>
> • **Tickets Attributable to Software Defects:** 4 (8.51%)
>
> • **Mean Time to Resolution (All Tickets):** 4.7 business days
>
> • **Mean Time to Resolution (SLA-Compliant Tickets):** 2.3 business days

The data demonstrates that the overwhelming majority of support issues reported by Arcadia were routine in nature --- relating to system configuration adjustments, user training gaps, and environmental setup --- and were resolved within the Service Level Agreement (\"SLA\") timeframes established in Exhibit D of the Master Software License and Services Agreement (\"MSLSA\") dated March 15, 2022. A significant subset of tickets (12 of 47, or approximately 25.5%) were traced to data integrity errors originating in legacy source files migrated by Arcadia\'s third-party systems integrator, Linden Park Consulting, LLC. Only four (4) tickets involved confirmed software defects in the NexusCore platform, all of which were remediated through software patches released on May 2, 2023, and June 19, 2023 --- both dates falling within the 90-day limited warranty period that expired on April 15, 2023, or shortly thereafter as part of Meridian\'s ongoing maintenance obligations under the MSLSA.

**[2. SLA Framework and Severity Classification]{.underline}**

The MSLSA Exhibit D establishes the following severity classifications and corresponding SLA response and resolution targets:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Severity Level**     **Definition**                                                                                  **Response Time SLA**   **Resolution Time SLA**
  ---------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ----------------------- -------------------------
  **SEV-1 (Critical)**   Production system down; no workaround available; materially impacts patient-facing operations   1 hour                  8 business hours

  **SEV-2 (High)**       Major feature impaired; workaround available but degraded functionality                         4 hours                 3 business days

  **SEV-3 (Medium)**     Minor feature impaired; workaround available; limited user impact                               8 hours                 7 business days

  **SEV-4 (Low)**        Cosmetic issue, documentation question, or enhancement request                                  1 business day          15 business days
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For purposes of SLA compliance measurement, \"Response Time\" is measured from ticket submission to first substantive acknowledgment by a Meridian support engineer, and \"Resolution Time\" is measured from ticket submission to confirmed resolution or delivery of a viable workaround accepted by the Client.

**[3. Ticket Distribution Summary]{.underline}**

**3.1 Distribution by Category**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Category**                                **Count**         **Percentage**    **Avg. Resolution (Business Days)**
  ------------------------------------------- ----------------- ----------------- -------------------------------------
  Routine --- Configuration / Environment     18                38.30%            1.9

  Routine --- User Training / Knowledge Gap   9                 19.15%            1.4

  Routine --- Reporting Template Adjustment   4                 8.51%             3.8

  Data Quality --- Legacy Migration Error     12                25.53%            8.6

  Software Defect --- NexusCore Platform      4                 8.51%             14.5

  **Total**                                   **47**            **100.00%**       **4.7**
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**3.2 Distribution by Severity Level**

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Severity**       **Count**      **Within SLA**   **Outside SLA**   **SLA Compliance Rate**
  ------------------ -------------- ---------------- ----------------- -------------------------
  SEV-1 (Critical)   2              1                1                 50.00%

  SEV-2 (High)       8              5                3                 62.50%

  SEV-3 (Medium)     22             20               2                 90.91%

  SEV-4 (Low)        15             15               0                 100.00%

  **Total**          **47**         **31**           **16**            **65.96%**
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Note:** Of the 16 tickets resolved outside SLA timeframes, 12 were data quality tickets for which the root cause lay outside Meridian\'s software and required coordination with Linden Park Consulting, LLC (Arcadia\'s systems integrator). The remaining 4 were software defect tickets requiring code-level investigation and patch development. When data quality tickets attributable to third-party migration errors are excluded from the SLA calculation, Meridian\'s SLA compliance rate for issues within its scope of responsibility was **31 of 35 tickets (88.57%)**.

**3.3 Distribution by Month**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Month**         **Tickets Opened**   **Tickets Closed**   **Cumulative Open**
  ----------------- -------------------- -------------------- ---------------------
  March 2023        14                   10                   4

  April 2023        12                   13                   3

  May 2023          9                    11                   1

  June 2023         7                    8                    0

  July 2023         5                    5                    0

  **Total**         **47**               **47**               **0**
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The data reflects a steadily declining ticket volume over the reporting period, consistent with normal post-Go-Live stabilization patterns. All 47 tickets were fully resolved by July 31, 2023.

**[4. Category A: Routine Tickets Resolved Within SLA (31 Tickets)]{.underline}**

The following 31 tickets were resolved within applicable SLA timeframes. These are organized into three subcategories: Configuration/Environment (18), User Training/Knowledge Gap (9), and Reporting Template Adjustment (4).

**4.1 Configuration and Environment Issues (18 Tickets)**

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Ticket ID**   **Date Opened**   **Date Closed**   **Severity**   **Description**                                                             **Root Cause**                                                                             **Resolution Time**
  --------------- ----------------- ----------------- -------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ ---------------------
  ARC-1001        03/08/2023        03/09/2023        SEV-3          Dashboard load times exceeding 15 seconds on intermittent basis             Client browser cache settings; proxy configuration not optimized for NexusCore             1 day

  ARC-1002        03/08/2023        03/10/2023        SEV-3          Report scheduler failing for nightly batch jobs                             Cron job configuration pointed to deprecated API endpoint after Go-Live cutover            2 days

  ARC-1004        03/11/2023        03/13/2023        SEV-3          Single sign-on (SSO) timeout set to 15 min; client requests 60 min          SSO configuration parameter adjustment per client security policy                          2 days

  ARC-1007        03/15/2023        03/16/2023        SEV-4          User role permissions not reflecting updated organizational hierarchy       Arcadia HR changes not propagated to NexusCore LDAP sync                                   1 day

  ARC-1009        03/19/2023        03/19/2023        SEV-4          Email notification templates displaying incorrect facility name             Tenant configuration metadata field contained legacy \"HealthBridge\" reference; updated   0.5 days

  ARC-1011        03/22/2023        03/23/2023        SEV-4          PDF export formatting misaligned for custom report                          Print stylesheet not configured for Arcadia\'s custom template dimensions                  1 day

  ARC-1014        03/28/2023        03/29/2023        SEV-4          API rate limiting triggering on batch data pulls                            Client integration script not implementing pagination; guidance provided                   1 day

  ARC-1018        04/05/2023        04/07/2023        SEV-3          Audit log timestamps displaying in UTC rather than Central Time             Timezone configuration for audit subsystem set to default; corrected to CT                 2 days

  ARC-1020        04/10/2023        04/11/2023        SEV-4          Data retention policy not configured for HIPAA compliance intervals         Client had not submitted data retention policy matrix; configured upon receipt             1 day

  ARC-1022        04/14/2023        04/17/2023        SEV-3          Analytics module not recognizing ICD-10 code updates from Q1 2023           Code table update package not applied during scheduled maintenance window; applied         3 days

  ARC-1025        04/21/2023        04/22/2023        SEV-4          Duplicate alert notifications sent to administrators                        Alert deduplication threshold set too low; adjusted from 5 min to 30 min window            1 day

  ARC-1027        04/28/2023        04/28/2023        SEV-4          Custom field labels truncated in mobile view                                Responsive design breakpoint adjustment for client-specific field lengths                  0.5 days

  ARC-1030        05/08/2023        05/10/2023        SEV-3          SFTP integration dropping connection during large file transfers            Client firewall timeout set to 120 seconds; recommended increase to 600 seconds            2 days

  ARC-1033        05/18/2023        05/19/2023        SEV-4          Dashboard widget for bed utilization showing \"N/A\" for three facilities   Facility IDs in configuration table had trailing whitespace characters                     1 day

  ARC-1036        06/02/2023        06/05/2023        SEV-3          Scheduled reports not including newly onboarded clinic locations            Clinic locations added after Go-Live required manual inclusion in report scope             3 days

  ARC-1039        06/15/2023        06/16/2023        SEV-4          User unable to export data to CSV format                                    Browser extension conflict; resolved with client IT team                                   1 day

  ARC-1042        07/03/2023        07/05/2023        SEV-3          Performance degradation during peak usage (8--10 AM CT)                     Auto-scaling configuration not tuned for Arcadia\'s usage profile; adjusted                2 days

  ARC-1045        07/18/2023        07/19/2023        SEV-4          Help documentation links returning 404 errors                               Documentation portal URL path updated in v2.3.1; client bookmarks stale                    1 day
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Summary:** All 18 configuration/environment tickets were classified SEV-3 or SEV-4 and resolved within 0.5 to 3 business days, well within applicable SLA windows. Root causes were uniformly attributable to standard post-deployment configuration tuning, client-side environment settings, or administrative actions required from Arcadia\'s IT staff.

**4.2 User Training and Knowledge Gap Issues (9 Tickets)**

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Ticket ID**   **Date Opened**   **Date Closed**   **Severity**   **Description**                                                               **Root Cause**                                                                                                                         **Resolution Time**
  --------------- ----------------- ----------------- -------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------
  ARC-1003        03/09/2023        03/10/2023        SEV-4          Users unable to locate predictive analytics dashboard                         Feature located under \"Advanced Analytics\" menu; users trained on standard menu only                                                 1 day

  ARC-1008        03/16/2023        03/17/2023        SEV-4          \"How do I create a custom cohort analysis?\" inquiry from clinical analyst   Not a defect; user training documentation provided and walkthrough scheduled                                                           1 day

  ARC-1012        03/23/2023        03/24/2023        SEV-4          Report builder producing unexpected aggregation results                       User applying incorrect filter logic; resolved via training session                                                                    1 day

  ARC-1016        04/02/2023        04/03/2023        SEV-4          Bulk upload feature \"not working\"                                           User attempting to upload .xlsx format; system requires .csv per documentation                                                         1 day

  ARC-1019        04/08/2023        04/10/2023        SEV-3          Clinical team reports \"data mismatch\" between NexusCore and source EHR      User comparing real-time EHR feed against NexusCore\'s nightly batch refresh cycle; expected behavior documented in User Guide § 4.3   2 days

  ARC-1024        04/18/2023        04/19/2023        SEV-4          \"System is slow\" complaint from single user                                 User\'s workstation had insufficient RAM (4 GB); minimum requirement is 8 GB per system requirements document                          1 day

  ARC-1031        05/10/2023        05/12/2023        SEV-3          Administrative user unable to configure new role permissions                  User did not have \"Super Admin\" role; Arcadia IT had not provisioned per internal policy                                             2 days

  ARC-1038        06/12/2023        06/13/2023        SEV-4          Request for additional training on report scheduling module                   Enhancement request / training need; additional training session delivered                                                             1 day

  ARC-1044        07/14/2023        07/15/2023        SEV-4          Users report \"missing data\" in quarterly summary                            Data present but users applying incorrect date range filter (fiscal vs. calendar quarter)                                              1 day
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Summary:** All 9 user training tickets were classified SEV-3 or SEV-4 and resolved within 1 to 2 business days. None involved software defects. Root causes were consistently attributable to gaps in user knowledge, failure to consult available documentation, or client-side administrative oversights. It is noteworthy that Change Order CO-003, executed on November 1, 2022, provided \$350,000 in extended training services, and Arcadia was advised during the training program that ongoing user education would be necessary as staff turnover occurred.

**4.3 Reporting Template Adjustment Issues (4 Tickets)**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Ticket ID**   **Date Opened**   **Date Closed**   **Severity**   **Description**                                                            **Root Cause**                                                                    **Resolution Time**
  --------------- ----------------- ----------------- -------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------
  ARC-1006        03/14/2023        03/17/2023        SEV-3          Readmission rate report template not matching Arcadia\'s internal format   Template customization required; original SOW specified standard templates only   3 days

  ARC-1023        04/16/2023        04/20/2023        SEV-3          Patient volume trending report needs additional breakout by payer type     Feature enhancement beyond original scope; delivered as goodwill adjustment       4 days

  ARC-1035        05/25/2023        05/31/2023        SEV-3          Regulatory compliance report needs updated CMS measure definitions         CMS updated measure specifications in Q1 2023; template refresh applied           4 days

  ARC-1041        06/28/2023        07/03/2023        SEV-3          Executive dashboard requests additional KPI widgets                        Scope expansion; configured as standard support request within SLA                5 days
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Summary:** These 4 tickets involved requests for report template modifications or updates. All were resolved within the 7-business-day SLA for SEV-3 tickets. Notably, tickets ARC-1023 and ARC-1041 involved feature enhancements beyond the original SOW scope that Meridian fulfilled as goodwill accommodations without requiring a Change Order.

**[5. Category B: Data Quality Issues --- Legacy Migration Errors (12 Tickets)]{.underline}**

The following 12 tickets were attributable to data integrity errors in legacy data migrated from Arcadia\'s prior platform, HealthBridge 4.0. Pursuant to Section 3.2 of SOW-1, Arcadia was solely responsible for all data migration activities, and Arcadia retained Linden Park Consulting, LLC of Houston, Texas as its third-party systems integrator to perform the data extraction, transformation, and loading (\"ETL\") process. Meridian\'s contractual role with respect to data migration was limited to advisory consulting services.

In each case below, Meridian\'s support engineering team identified the root cause as corrupted, incomplete, or improperly transformed data residing in the NexusCore database as a direct result of the Linden Park migration. These tickets were resolved outside standard SLA timeframes because remediation required (a) root cause analysis to distinguish data-layer issues from application-layer issues, (b) coordination with Linden Park and/or Arcadia\'s internal data team, and (c) in several cases, re-extraction and re-loading of source data from HealthBridge 4.0.

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Ticket ID**   **Date Opened**   **Date Closed**   **Severity**   **Description**                                                                                **Root Cause Detail**                                                                                                                                                                   **Resolution Time**
  --------------- ----------------- ----------------- -------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------
  ARC-1005        03/12/2023        03/27/2023        SEV-2          Patient demographic records showing duplicate entries (approx. 14,200 duplicate records)       Linden Park ETL script lacked deduplication logic for patient MRN field; duplicate source records loaded without validation                                                             11 days

  ARC-1010        03/20/2023        04/04/2023        SEV-2          Historical claims data missing for Q3--Q4 2021                                                 Linden Park extraction query used incorrect date range filter; data existed in HealthBridge 4.0 but was not extracted                                                                   11 days

  ARC-1013        03/26/2023        04/06/2023        SEV-2          ICD-10 codes truncated to 5 characters (should be 7)                                           Linden Park transformation mapping truncated code field to VARCHAR(5) instead of VARCHAR(7)                                                                                             9 days

  ARC-1015        03/30/2023        04/11/2023        SEV-2          Provider NPI numbers misaligned with facility records                                          Source data join in Linden Park ETL script used incorrect key relationship; NPI-to-facility mapping corrupted                                                                           8 days

  ARC-1017        04/03/2023        04/14/2023        SEV-2          Financial data for 3 hospitals showing zero balances                                           Linden Park load script skipped records with null cost center codes; approximately \$8.2M in historical charges not migrated                                                            9 days

  ARC-1021        04/12/2023        04/24/2023        SEV-1          Real-time analytics dashboard producing materially inaccurate patient census numbers           Bed census data migrated with incorrect facility ID mappings; 6 of 23 hospitals reporting data under wrong facility codes                                                               8 days

  ARC-1026        04/25/2023        05/08/2023        SEV-2          Readmission rate calculations inflated by approximately 18%                                    Duplicate encounter records loaded by Linden Park; overlapping admission/discharge timestamps not deduplicated                                                                          9 days

  ARC-1029        05/04/2023        05/17/2023        SEV-2          Payer mix analysis showing \"Unknown\" payer for 22% of records                                Linden Park mapping table did not include 14 regional payer codes specific to Louisiana facilities                                                                                      9 days

  ARC-1032        05/15/2023        05/24/2023        SEV-3          Historical quality metric trends inconsistent with source system reports                       Date/time fields migrated without timezone normalization; HealthBridge stored in local time, NexusCore requires UTC                                                                     7 days

  ARC-1034        05/22/2023        06/02/2023        SEV-3          Lab result data showing mismatched units of measurement                                        Linden Park transformation did not apply unit conversion for legacy lab systems using non-standard coding                                                                               8 days

  ARC-1037        06/08/2023        06/19/2023        SEV-2          Revenue cycle data for 2022 showing \$3.1M discrepancy against Arcadia\'s audited financials   Linden Park extraction omitted adjustment entries and write-offs from HealthBridge general ledger module                                                                                8 days

  ARC-1040        06/22/2023        07/03/2023        SEV-3          Clinical outcome metrics not matching values reported to CMS                                   Source data integrity issue: HealthBridge 4.0 contained known data quality issues acknowledged by Arcadia in pre-migration assessment; Linden Park did not apply data cleansing rules   8 days
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**5.1 Root Cause Analysis Summary --- Data Quality Tickets**

All 12 data quality tickets share a common root cause chain:

1\. **Source Data Integrity:** The legacy HealthBridge 4.0 system contained pre-existing data quality issues, including duplicate records, inconsistent coding, and incomplete historical data. Arcadia\'s own pre-migration data assessment (conducted by Linden Park in August 2022) identified 37 data quality risks. Of these, only 19 were remediated prior to migration.

2\. **ETL Script Errors:** Linden Park Consulting\'s ETL scripts contained multiple defects, including incorrect field mappings, insufficient deduplication logic, improper date range filters, and missing transformation rules for regional coding variations.

3\. **Insufficient Validation:** Linden Park\'s migration validation procedures did not include comprehensive record count reconciliation, referential integrity checks, or statistical sampling of migrated data against source records.

4\. **Contractual Responsibility:** Section 3.2 of SOW-1 expressly assigns sole responsibility for data migration to Arcadia. Meridian\'s role was limited to advisory consulting. Change Order CO-004, executed December 5, 2022, in the amount of \$900,000, was specifically scoped to provide data remediation services to address errors introduced during the Linden Park migration --- further confirming the parties\' contemporaneous understanding that migration defects were not attributable to Meridian\'s software or implementation services.

**Critical Observation:** The 12 data quality tickets represent 75% (12 of 16) of all tickets resolved outside SLA timeframes. When these tickets are excluded from SLA compliance calculations, Meridian\'s SLA performance for issues within its contractual scope of responsibility was **31 of 35 tickets resolved within SLA (88.57%)**.

**[6. Category C: Software Defect Tickets (4 Tickets)]{.underline}**

The following 4 tickets involved confirmed software defects in the NexusCore platform. These were the only tickets in the reporting period attributable to Meridian\'s software code. All 4 defects were remediated through two software patch releases.

**6.1 Defects Remediated in Patch v2.3.1 (Released May 2, 2023)**

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Ticket ID**   **Date Opened**   **Date Closed**   **Severity**   **Description**                                                                                                                **Root Cause Detail**                                                                                                                                                                                  **Resolution Time**
  --------------- ----------------- ----------------- -------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ ---------------------
  ARC-1028        05/01/2023        05/09/2023        SEV-1          Report generation engine intermittently returns HTTP 500 error under concurrent user load exceeding 75 simultaneous sessions   Memory leak in report rendering service caused by improper garbage collection in NexusCore Analytics Engine v2.3.0; heap allocation exceeded container memory limits under sustained concurrent load   6 days

  ARC-1043        05/01/2023        05/09/2023        SEV-2          Scheduled report delivery via SFTP intermittently fails with authentication timeout                                            SFTP client library (internal dependency) contained race condition in connection pool management; connections not properly released after timeout events                                               6 days
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Patch v2.3.1 Details:**

> • **Release Date:** May 2, 2023
>
> • **Deployment to Arcadia Environment:** May 2, 2023 (same day; automatic cloud deployment)
>
> • **Validation Complete:** May 9, 2023 (Arcadia confirmed resolution)
>
> • **Scope:** Resolved memory leak in report rendering service; upgraded SFTP client library to v4.1.2 with connection pool fix
>
> • **Impact Assessment:** The memory leak (ARC-1028) was the more impactful defect. It manifested only when concurrent report generation sessions exceeded 75, which occurred during Arcadia\'s peak morning reporting window (8:00--10:00 AM CT). Arcadia reported this issue as occurring \"intermittently,\" consistent with the load-dependent trigger. The SFTP issue (ARC-1043) affected scheduled overnight report deliveries, with approximately 12% of scheduled deliveries failing during the affected period.

**6.2 Defects Remediated in Patch v2.3.2 (Released June 19, 2023)**

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Ticket ID**   **Date Opened**   **Date Closed**   **Severity**   **Description**                                                                                                            **Root Cause Detail**                                                                                                                                                                                           **Resolution Time**
  --------------- ----------------- ----------------- -------------- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------
  ARC-1046        06/05/2023        06/27/2023        SEV-2          Data aggregation calculations in population health module producing rounding errors in decimal precision beyond 4 places   Floating-point arithmetic in aggregation engine using IEEE 754 double precision instead of BigDecimal for financial/clinical metric calculations; cumulative rounding variance up to 0.003% in large datasets   16 days

  ARC-1047        06/10/2023        06/27/2023        SEV-3          API response payloads for bulk data export endpoint include deprecated field names from pre-v2.0 schema                    API versioning logic failed to strip legacy field mappings when tenant was provisioned on v2.3.x; backward-compatibility layer inadvertently included                                                           12 days
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Patch v2.3.2 Details:**

> • **Release Date:** June 19, 2023
>
> • **Deployment to Arcadia Environment:** June 19, 2023 (automatic cloud deployment)
>
> • **Validation Complete:** June 27, 2023 (Arcadia confirmed resolution)
>
> • **Scope:** Migrated aggregation engine to BigDecimal arithmetic for all clinical and financial metric calculations; corrected API versioning logic to properly scope field mappings by tenant provisioning version
>
> • **Impact Assessment:** The rounding error (ARC-1046) produced a maximum cumulative variance of 0.003% across large dataset aggregations. While technically a defect, the variance was well within acceptable tolerances for clinical reporting and would not have produced materially inaccurate results. The API field name issue (ARC-1047) was a cosmetic/compatibility issue affecting downstream integrations that relied on specific field names; it did not affect data accuracy.

**6.3 Software Defect Summary**

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Defect**                       **Severity**   **Materiality**                                            **Patch**   **Days to Patch Release**   **Days to Validated Resolution**
  -------------------------------- -------------- ---------------------------------------------------------- ----------- --------------------------- ----------------------------------
  Memory leak (ARC-1028)           SEV-1          Moderate --- affected peak-hour report generation          v2.3.1      1 day                       8 days

  SFTP race condition (ARC-1043)   SEV-2          Low --- affected \~12% of scheduled overnight deliveries   v2.3.1      1 day                       8 days

  Rounding error (ARC-1046)        SEV-2          Minimal --- 0.003% variance                                v2.3.2      14 days                     22 days

  API field names (ARC-1047)       SEV-3          Cosmetic --- deprecated field names included               v2.3.2      9 days                      17 days
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Key Observations:**

1\. Only 4 of 47 tickets (8.51%) involved actual NexusCore software defects.

2\. None of the 4 defects caused system downtime, data loss, or corruption of patient data.

3\. The most significant defect (memory leak, ARC-1028) was load-dependent and intermittent, consistent with Arcadia\'s characterization of \"intermittent latency issues\" in Dr. Okonkwo\'s March 8, 2023 email to Sandra Bledsoe.

4\. All defects were remediated through standard software maintenance processes. The Section 9.1 limited warranty period ran from Go-Live (January 15, 2023) through April 15, 2023. The first two defects were patched on May 2, 2023, which is 17 days after the warranty period closed; however, ongoing maintenance and support obligations under the MSLSA (for which Arcadia paid \$1,900,000 in Year 1 maintenance fees) independently obligated Meridian to provide bug fixes and patches.

5\. The nature and severity of these defects are consistent with standard post-deployment software stabilization and do not support the characterization in the First Amended Complaint that the NexusCore software was \"materially defective\" or \"unfit for its intended purpose.\"

**[7. Aggregate Analysis and Conclusions]{.underline}**

**7.1 Overall Ticket Resolution Performance**

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Metric**                                   **Value**
  -------------------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Total Tickets                                47

  Resolved Within SLA (All Categories)         31 (65.96%)

  Resolved Within SLA (Meridian-Scope Only)    31 of 35 (88.57%)

  Mean Time to Resolution (All)                4.7 business days

  Mean Time to Resolution (Routine)            2.3 business days

  Mean Time to Resolution (Data Quality)       8.6 business days

  Mean Time to Resolution (Software Defects)   14.5 business days

  Zero-Day / Same-Day Resolutions              3

  Tickets Requiring Patch Release              4

  Tickets Requiring Linden Park Coordination   12
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**7.2 Root Cause Distribution**

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Root Cause Category**                   **Tickets**       **% of Total**    **Responsible Party**
  ----------------------------------------- ----------------- ----------------- ----------------------------------------
  Client-side configuration / environment   18                38.30%            Arcadia / Arcadia IT

  User error / training gap                 9                 19.15%            Arcadia end users

  Report template customization requests    4                 8.51%             N/A (enhancement requests)

  Legacy data migration errors              12                25.53%            Linden Park Consulting (Arcadia\'s SI)

  NexusCore software defects                4                 8.51%             Meridian

  **Total**                                 **47**            **100.00%**       
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**7.3 Key Conclusions**

1\. **Software Stability:** The NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module demonstrated strong post-Go-Live stability. Only 4 of 47 tickets (8.51%) were attributable to software defects, and none resulted in sustained system outage, data loss, or material impairment to Arcadia\'s clinical operations.

2\. **Data Migration --- Not a Meridian Issue:** The single largest category of problematic tickets (12 of 47, or 25.53%) is directly traceable to data migration errors caused by Linden Park Consulting, LLC, the third-party systems integrator retained by Arcadia. Section 3.2 of SOW-1 assigns sole responsibility for data migration to Arcadia. Meridian\'s contractual role was limited to advisory consulting (\$1,600,000 of the \$14,700,000 MSLSA value). The execution of Change Order CO-004 on December 5, 2022 --- a \$900,000 engagement for data remediation services to address Linden Park migration errors --- further confirms that both parties understood data migration failures were Arcadia\'s responsibility.

3\. **Acceptance Testing Waiver:** Arcadia did not deliver written notice of material nonconformity within the 30-day acceptance testing window that closed on February 14, 2023. Under Section 5.3 of the MSLSA, acceptance was deemed granted. Arcadia\'s first written complaint was the March 8, 2023, email from Dr. Okonkwo --- 22 days after the acceptance window closed. The issues identified in that email (\"intermittent latency issues\" and \"occasional report generation errors\") are consistent with the SEV-1 memory leak defect (ARC-1028, opened May 1, 2023) and the data quality issues, neither of which constitutes a material nonconformity in the NexusCore software as delivered.

4\. **Declining Trend:** The ticket volume declined from 14 in March 2023 to 5 in July 2023, and all 47 tickets were fully closed by July 31, 2023. This trajectory is consistent with normal enterprise software post-deployment stabilization and contradicts the complaint\'s characterization of an ongoing, systemic product failure.

5\. **Damages Attribution:** The support ticket record does not support Arcadia\'s claim of \$47,300,000 in damages. The vast majority of issues were routine post-deployment items or were caused by Arcadia\'s own third-party vendor. The 4 software defects were patched promptly and had minimal operational impact.

**[8. Appendix: Complete Ticket Log]{.underline}**

The complete ticket log with full resolution notes, email correspondence, and engineering analysis for all 47 tickets is maintained in Meridian\'s Zenith Service Management Platform under Arcadia account ID ARC-HC-2022-0315. Full export has been preserved pursuant to the litigation hold notice issued August 15, 2023, and produced to Stonebridge & Calloway LLP as MERID-SUPP-000001 through MERID-SUPP-002847 (2,847 pages).

**--- END OF REPORT ---**

This report was prepared at the direction of Stonebridge & Calloway LLP for use in connection with Arcadia Health Systems, LLC v. Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc., Case No. 1:23-cv-00847-CMA (W.D. Tex.). This document is protected by the attorney-client privilege and the work product doctrine. Distribution is limited to authorized recipients. No portion of this report may be disclosed to third parties without the prior written consent of Stonebridge & Calloway LLP.

**Prepared By:**

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Sandra Bledsoe Vice President, Customer Success Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc. 9200 Research Boulevard, Suite 400 Austin, TX 78759

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

**EXHIBIT D**

**SELECTED EMAIL CORRESPONDENCE** **MSLSA CONTRACT NEGOTIATION PERIOD** **FEBRUARY 22, 2022 -- MARCH 14, 2022**

*Produced by Defendant Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.* *Bates Nos. MCS-00004217 through MCS-00004248*

**FROM:** Jonathan Breckenridge \\\<jbreckenridge@wexfordhale.com\\\> **TO:** David Arsenault \\\<darsenault@stonebridgecalloway.com\\\> **CC:** Dr. Rachel Okonkwo \\\<rokonkwo@arcadiahealth.com\\\>; Martin Schreiber \\\<mschreiber@arcadiahealth.com\\\> **DATE:** February 22, 2022, 9:14 AM CST **SUBJECT:** Arcadia Health Systems / Meridian Cloud Solutions --- Draft MSLSA Review **ATTACHMENTS:** Arcadia\\*MSLSA\\*Redline\\\_v1.docx (148 KB)

David,

Good morning. Our firm has been engaged by Arcadia Health Systems, LLC to represent it in connection with the proposed Master Software License and Services Agreement and Statement of Work with your client, Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc. I understand from Dr. Okonkwo and Martin Schreiber that you will be handling this matter on the Meridian side under Meg Calloway\'s supervision. Please confirm.

Attached is our initial redline of the draft MSLSA that Meridian circulated on February 15. We have a number of comments across the document, but I want to flag several priority issues up front so we can begin substantive discussions promptly. In no particular order:

1\. **Limitation of Liability / Consequential Damages Waiver (Sections 8.1--8.3).** The mutual consequential damages waiver in Section 8.2 is extremely broad and, in our view, inequitable as applied to a licensee that is making a \$14.7 million commitment and transitioning off a legacy platform. We have proposed deletion of the consequential damages waiver in its entirety, or in the alternative, significant carve-outs for data loss, business interruption directly caused by software defects, and costs of re-implementation. Our client\'s exposure in the event of a failed implementation is asymmetrically higher than Meridian\'s, and the risk allocation should reflect that reality.

2\. **Warranty Terms (Section 9.1).** The 90-day limited warranty running from Go-Live is inadequate for an enterprise deployment of this scale. We have proposed a 12-month warranty period with remedies that include, at Licensee\'s election, either (a) repair/replacement, (b) a full refund of all fees paid, or (c) damages for cost of cover. We have also proposed deletion of the warranty disclaimer in Section 9.4 as it pertains to fitness for a particular purpose --- Arcadia is licensing NexusCore specifically for healthcare analytics, and Meridian is fully aware of that particular purpose.

3\. **Acceptance Testing (Section 5.3).** The current draft provides for \"deemed acceptance\" after 15 business days with no written procedures, criteria, or cure period. This is insufficient. We have proposed a detailed acceptance testing framework that includes defined acceptance criteria tied to the Documentation, a structured deficiency notice process, a 30-calendar-day testing window, and a cure period before deemed acceptance can take effect.

4\. **Forum Selection (Section 12.8).** The current draft designates Delaware courts as the exclusive forum. Arcadia is a Texas company, all of its operations are in Texas and Louisiana, the software will be deployed in Texas, and the Meridian team servicing this account is based in Austin. We propose that jurisdiction be vested exclusively in the federal or state courts located in Travis County, Texas.

5\. **Integration Clause (Section 12.1).** We have no objection to a standard integration clause in principle, but want to ensure it is not used as a shield against pre-contractual representations that Meridian\'s sales team has made to our client. We have proposed a carve-out for fraudulent misrepresentations.

We look forward to your response and are available for a call at your convenience to discuss.

Best regards,

Jonathan Breckenridge Partner Wexford Hale LLP 300 West 6th Street, Suite 1500 Austin, TX 78701 (512) 555-8100

*MCS-00004219*

**FROM:** David Arsenault \\\<darsenault@stonebridgecalloway.com\\\> **TO:** Jonathan Breckenridge \\\<jbreckenridge@wexfordhale.com\\\> **CC:** Margaret Calloway \\\<mcalloway@stonebridgecalloway.com\\\>; Patricia M. Espinoza \\\<pespinoza@meridiancloud.com\\\> **DATE:** February 24, 2022, 3:47 PM CST **SUBJECT:** RE: Arcadia Health Systems / Meridian Cloud Solutions --- Draft MSLSA Review **ATTACHMENTS:** Meridian\\*MSLSA\\*Response\\\_v2.docx (152 KB)

Jonathan,

Thank you for the redline and for flagging the priority items. I can confirm that I am handling this matter day-to-day, with Meg Calloway supervising. Patricia Espinoza from Meridian\'s legal department is copied and will be involved as needed.

We have reviewed your redline carefully with our client. Attached is Meridian\'s response markup. Let me address your priority items in turn:

**1. Limitation of Liability / Consequential Damages Waiver (Sections 8.1--8.3).**

This is a non-starter for Meridian, and I want to be transparent about that from the outset so we do not spend weeks negotiating a position that will not change. The mutual consequential damages waiver is a foundational element of Meridian\'s risk allocation framework across all of its enterprise licensing agreements. Meridian licenses NexusCore to clients across healthcare, financial services, and logistics, and the consequential damages waiver ensures predictability and symmetry in risk exposure. We cannot agree to delete it or to carve out categories of consequential damages such as lost profits, business interruption, or re-implementation costs --- each of those is precisely the type of indirect, consequential damage that the waiver is designed to address.

I would note that the waiver is mutual. Meridian also gives up the right to claim consequential damages against Arcadia. The aggregate liability cap in Section 8.1, which limits each party\'s total liability to fees paid or payable during the twelve-month period immediately preceding the event giving rise to liability, provides Arcadia with a meaningful damages recovery path for direct damages in any breach scenario, while ensuring that neither party faces unbounded exposure.

Meridian has not agreed to remove or modify this provision in any of its enterprise license agreements, and it will not do so here. We understand that Arcadia views its risk as asymmetric, but the pricing of the agreement already reflects the risk allocation established by these provisions --- any change to the liability structure would necessarily implicate a repricing of the entire deal.

**2. Warranty Terms (Section 9.1).**

We can discuss the warranty terms, but a 12-month warranty period is not feasible. Meridian\'s standard is 90 days from Go-Live, which is consistent with industry practice for enterprise SaaS deployments. We are willing to discuss the *remedy* structure within that 90-day window --- specifically, we can agree that if Meridian is unable to cure a material nonconformity within 60 days of written notice, Licensee would be entitled to a pro-rata refund of pre-paid license fees for the unexpired portion of the term. But we cannot extend the warranty period itself, and we cannot agree to a \"cost of cover\" remedy --- that is functionally a consequential damages claim repackaged as a warranty remedy.

Regarding the implied warranty disclaimer in Section 9.4: this stays. The express warranty in Section 9.1 defines the scope of Meridian\'s quality commitment --- the software will perform substantially in accordance with the Documentation. That is a meaningful, testable standard. Meridian is not in a position to warrant fitness for a particular purpose because the ultimate fitness of any software deployment depends on implementation decisions, data quality, system integration, and other factors outside Meridian\'s control. This is especially true here, where Arcadia will be responsible for its own data migration and will be retaining a third-party systems integrator.

**3. Acceptance Testing (Section 5.3).**

This is an area where we can find common ground. We agree that the current 15-business-day deemed acceptance provision could be improved. We are amenable to a 30-calendar-day acceptance testing window following deployment of each module, with acceptance criteria tied to the Documentation. We have proposed language in the attached markup. We do want to preserve the deemed acceptance mechanism --- if Licensee does not deliver written notice of material nonconformity within the 30-day window, acceptance is deemed granted. This is critical for project timeline certainty. We are not willing to add an open-ended cure period that would delay acceptance indefinitely.

**4. Forum Selection (Section 12.8).**

We hear you on forum selection. Meridian\'s preference is Delaware (given its state of incorporation and the Delaware governing law clause), but we understand the practical considerations. We can agree to Austin, Travis County, Texas --- federal or state courts --- as the exclusive forum, provided that the governing law clause (Section 12.7) remains Delaware law. This gives Arcadia the convenience of a Texas forum while preserving the substantive law that Meridian\'s commercial agreements are structured around. Agreed?

**5. Integration Clause (Section 12.1).**

We cannot agree to a fraud carve-out to the integration clause. The integration clause is standard and serves a critical function --- it defines the four corners of the parties\' agreement. Adding a carve-out for \"fraudulent misrepresentations\" would invite collateral disputes over the content and intent of pre-contractual communications and would undermine the certainty that the integration clause is designed to provide. If there are specific representations that Arcadia wants memorialized, the proper mechanism is to include them as express warranties in the agreement itself. We are happy to discuss any additional express warranty language Arcadia would like to propose.

Let me know when you are available for a call. Meg Calloway and Patricia Espinoza can both make themselves available on our end.

Best,

David Arsenault Senior Associate Stonebridge & Calloway LLP 600 Congress Avenue, Suite 2800 Austin, TX 78701 (512) 555-7200

David,

Thank you for the detailed response. We have discussed internally with our client and I want to share where Arcadia stands.

**1. Consequential Damages Waiver.** We are disappointed but not surprised by your position. Our client understands that this is a deal-level decision. Dr. Okonkwo and Martin Schreiber have reviewed the risk allocation carefully and, after internal deliberation, are prepared to accept the mutual consequential damages waiver **as drafted** --- including the waiver of lost profits, goodwill, data loss, and other intangible losses --- on the condition that the remaining open items are resolved to Arcadia\'s satisfaction. I want to be clear for the record: Arcadia is accepting this risk allocation knowingly and with the benefit of legal counsel, and is relying on the express warranty, acceptance testing, and support provisions to protect its interests. We reserve the right to revisit if the overall package does not come together.

**2. Warranty.** We appreciate the movement on the remedy structure. We can accept a 90-day warranty period with the repair/replace remedy and the 60-day cure period leading to a pro-rata refund as the backstop. However, we want to ensure that \"perform substantially in accordance with the Documentation\" is a meaningful standard. Our redline proposes a definition of \"Material Nonconformity\" as \"any failure of the Software to perform a material function described in the Documentation that materially impairs Licensee\'s use of the Software for its intended purpose.\" Can we agree on that definition?

We will drop the cost-of-cover remedy request and the request to delete the Section 9.4 disclaimer. Martin Schreiber has confirmed that he is comfortable with the implied warranty disclaimer given the express warranty language and the acceptance testing procedures.

**3. Acceptance Testing.** Your proposed 30-calendar-day window with deemed acceptance is acceptable in principle. We have a few refinements:

> \(a\) Acceptance criteria should be defined by reference to specific test scenarios set forth in an exhibit (we will draft Exhibit E with proposed test cases).
>
> \(b\) Written notice of nonconformity must identify the specific Documentation requirement that is not met and include reasonable supporting detail (logs, screenshots, etc.).
>
> \(c\) If Licensee delivers timely notice of a Material Nonconformity, Licensor shall have 15 business days to cure. If uncured, Licensee may reject the module and the parties shall negotiate in good faith regarding remediation.
>
> \(d\) Failure to deliver written notice within the 30-day window constitutes deemed acceptance --- we agree to this mechanism.

We think this framework is fair and gives both sides clarity. It also incentivizes Arcadia to be diligent about testing, which should align with Meridian\'s interest in a smooth Go-Live.

**4. Forum Selection.** Agreed --- Austin, Travis County, Texas (federal or state courts), with Delaware governing law per Section 12.7. We have no objection to this pairing.

**5. Integration Clause.** We hear your position on the fraud carve-out. After discussion with our client, we will withdraw that request. Arcadia accepts the integration clause as drafted. Martin Schreiber has independently verified NexusCore\'s capabilities through reference calls and a proof-of-concept trial, and Arcadia is comfortable proceeding on the basis of the express contractual terms rather than pre-contractual representations.

We are converging. Can we schedule a call for Thursday to finalize the remaining open items? We have mark-ups on several other provisions (data security, indemnification, termination) that I would like to walk through efficiently.

Best,

Jonathan Breckenridge

One final note for the record. As we discussed on Monday\'s call, Arcadia is entering this agreement with eyes wide open regarding the risk allocation framework. The consequential damages waiver, the aggregate liability cap, the warranty limitations, and the deemed acceptance mechanism were all carefully considered by Arcadia\'s leadership and legal team. Arcadia\'s decision to proceed reflects its confidence in NexusCore based on the proof-of-concept results and reference checks, as well as its understanding that the contractual protections --- particularly the detailed acceptance testing procedures that we negotiated into Section 5.3 --- provide an adequate framework for identifying and resolving any issues during the critical post-deployment period. We expect Arcadia to be diligent in exercising those contractual rights, and we have counseled our client accordingly.

Thank you to you and David for a productive process. We look forward to a successful implementation.

Best regards,

Jonathan Breckenridge Partner Wexford Hale LLP 300 West 6th Street, Suite 1500 Austin, TX 78701 (512) 555-8100

*--- End of Exhibit D ---*

text: The contract negotiation email chain is devastating to Arcadia's fraud/reliance claims — Arcadia's own counsel memorialized that Arcadia accepted the consequential damages waiver "knowingly and with the benefit of legal counsel," withdrew the fraud carve-out to the integration clause, and relied on its own due diligence. The written proposal contains express disclaimers of "seamless" integration and "no reliance." Let me read the change orders (especially CO-004), the sales presentation, and the damages report to complete my picture.

result:
**CHANGE ORDER NO. CO-004**

**TO THE MASTER SOFTWARE LICENSE AND SERVICES AGREEMENT**

**AND STATEMENT OF WORK #1**

**Change Order Date:** December 5, 2022 **Change Order No.:** CO-004 **MSLSA Effective Date:** March 15, 2022 **SOW-1 Effective Date:** March 15, 2022

This Change Order No. CO-004 (\"**Change Order**\") is entered into as of December 5, 2022 (the \"**Change Order Effective Date**\"), by and between:

**Meridian Cloud Solutions, Inc.**, a Delaware corporation, with its principal place of business at 9200 Research Boulevard, Suite 400, Austin, TX 78759 (\"**Licensor**\" or \"**Meridian**\"),

and

**Arcadia Health Systems, LLC**, a Texas limited liability company, with its principal office at 4500 Medical Parkway, Suite 210, San Antonio, TX 78229 (\"**Licensee**\" or \"**Arcadia**\").

Licensor and Licensee are each referred to herein as a \"**Party**\" and collectively as the \"**Parties**.\"

This Change Order is issued pursuant to Section 5.4 (Change Orders) of the Master Software License and Services Agreement dated March 15, 2022, between the Parties (the \"**MSLSA**\"), and modifies the scope and fees of Statement of Work #1 executed concurrently therewith (\"**SOW-1**\"). Capitalized terms used but not defined herein shall have the meanings ascribed to them in the MSLSA or SOW-1, as applicable.

**1. Background and Purpose**

1.1 Under Section 3.2 of SOW-1, Licensee is solely responsible for all data migration activities, including extraction, transformation, and loading of legacy data from Licensee\'s existing HealthBridge 4.0 platform into the NexusCore Healthcare Analytics Module. Licensor\'s contractual obligation with respect to data migration is limited to advisory consulting services as described in Exhibit C to SOW-1.

1.2 In furtherance of its obligations under Section 3.2 of SOW-1, Licensee retained Linden Park Consulting, LLC, a Texas limited liability company (\"**Linden Park**\"), as its third-party systems integrator to design, develop, and execute data migration scripts and manage the extraction, transformation, and loading process.

1.3 During the extended implementation phase, Licensor\'s engineering team identified critical errors in data migration scripts prepared by Licensee\'s third-party systems integrator, Linden Park Consulting, LLC, which resulted in the following data integrity issues within the NexusCore staging environment:

> \(a\) Approximately 14,200 patient encounter records migrated with truncated or malformed diagnosis code fields, rendering the records unusable by the NexusCore analytics engine;
>
> \(b\) Duplicate record creation affecting approximately 8,700 unique provider identifiers due to faulty deduplication logic in Linden Park\'s ETL scripts;
>
> \(c\) Incorrect date-format mapping (MM/DD/YYYY vs. YYYY-MM-DD) across approximately 2.3 million transactional records migrated from HealthBridge 4.0, causing systemic errors in time-series reporting functions; and
>
> \(d\) Failure to map 37 legacy data fields identified in the Data Mapping Specification (Exhibit C-2 to SOW-1) to corresponding NexusCore schema fields, resulting in null-value population across multiple reporting dimensions.

1.4 Licensee has requested that Licensor perform data remediation services to identify, correct, and validate the errors described in Section 1.3 above, and Licensor has agreed to perform such services on the terms and conditions set forth in this Change Order.

**2. Scope of Data Remediation Services**

Licensor shall perform the following services (the \"**Data Remediation Services**\"):

> \(a\) **Diagnostic Assessment.** Comprehensive audit of all data migrated by Linden Park to identify the full extent of data integrity issues, including but not limited to those enumerated in Section 1.3.
>
> \(b\) **Script Remediation.** Development and execution of corrective transformation scripts to repair malformed, duplicated, and incorrectly mapped data records within the NexusCore staging and production environments.
>
> \(c\) **Data Validation.** Execution of automated and manual data validation protocols to verify the accuracy, completeness, and integrity of remediated data against source records extracted from HealthBridge 4.0.
>
> \(d\) **Reconciliation Reporting.** Delivery of a written reconciliation report documenting all corrections performed, records affected, and validation results.

**3. Fees.** The total fixed fee for the Data Remediation Services under this Change Order shall be **\$900,000.00** (nine hundred thousand dollars), payable as follows: fifty percent (50%) upon execution of this Change Order (\$450,000.00), and fifty percent (50%) upon Licensor\'s delivery of the reconciliation report described in Section 2(d) (\$450,000.00). All fees are subject to the payment terms of Section 6 of the MSLSA.

**4. Timeline.** Licensor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to complete the Data Remediation Services within forty-five (45) calendar days of the Change Order Effective Date.

**5. Acknowledgments.** 5.1 Licensee acknowledges and agrees that the data integrity issues described in Section 1.3 arose from errors in data migration scripts prepared and executed by Linden Park Consulting, LLC, and not from any defect in the NexusCore software or any act or omission of Licensor.

5.2 Nothing in this Change Order shall be construed as an assumption by Licensor of responsibility for data migration activities, which remain the sole responsibility of Licensee pursuant to Section 3.2 of SOW-1.

5.3 This Change Order does not modify, waive, or otherwise affect any limitation of liability, warranty disclaimer, or other provision of the MSLSA except as expressly set forth herein.

**6. General.** This Change Order is governed by and subject to the terms and conditions of the MSLSA. In the event of any conflict between this Change Order and the MSLSA, the MSLSA shall control except to the extent this Change Order expressly modifies the scope and fees of SOW-1. This Change Order may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original.

**SIGNATURE PAGE TO CHANGE ORDER NO. CO-004**

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF**, the Parties have caused this Change Order to be executed by their duly authorized representatives as of the Change Order Effective Date.
